$comment: DELIVERY and UPGRADE are capabilities, distinct from the capabilities they carry.
Neither has ever been proven for this plugin: --plugin-dir, which the per-capability
L4s use, loads from a source tree and bypasses the marketplace clone, marketplace.json
resolution, the version cache, installed_plugins.json, and scope/startup resolution.
Per the operator's Reading B ruling, releases MAY continue with delivery unproven.
THE VIOLATION IS SILENCE. scenarios/release-gate.mjs fails this repo's CI when
`delivery` is `unproven` and README.md does not carry `disclosure` verbatim, and
fails when `delivery` claims a proof that the committed record does not support.
`delivery` is not a dial. The gate DERIVES the true status from
scenarios/records/install-path.json and fails on any mismatch, so this field flips
to `proven` only, and automatically, when that record lands DEMONSTRATED and
version-bound. Editing it by hand goes red.
disclosure: Delivery is unproven: this plugin's install and upgrade path has never been verified end-to-end by a sandboxed proof. Per-capability L4s load the plugin from a source tree and therefore prove the capability, not its delivery.
